message:
feat(pool): Cx-aware cancellation for connection pool and semaphore acquire

Add cooperative cancellation support to the Pool and Semaphore layers so
that operations bail out with PoolError::Cancelled when their Cx context
is cancelled, instead of blocking until timeout or silently consuming
resources.

pool.rs:
- Add PoolError::Cancelled variant for explicit Cx cancellation
- Add checkpoint_explicit_cx() helper that maps Cx cancellation to
  PoolError::Cancelled
- try_acquire_with_cx(): checkpoint Cx before attempting acquire instead
  of the previous no-op cx::with_cx() call
- acquire_with_cx(): checkpoint Cx first, then use
  semaphore.acquire_owned_with_cx(cx) to propagate cancellation through
  the semaphore wait. Previously wrapped in cx::with_cx_async which did
  not actually propagate cancellation to the semaphore.
- Match on AcquireError::Closed vs AcquireError::Cancelled instead of
  treating all acquire failures as Closed
- Add 3 Cx cancellation tests: pre-cancelled try_acquire, pre-cancelled
  acquire (verifies no timeout increment), and cancel-while-waiting
  (verifies held permit is not affected)
- Add display/debug tests for Cancelled variant

runtime_compat.rs:
- Change AcquireError from unit struct to enum with Closed and Cancelled
  variants, enabling callers to distinguish cancellation from shutdown
- Add Semaphore::map_acquire_error() to translate asupersync's error
  variants including PolledAfterCompletion (mapped to Cancelled with
  debug_assert)
- Add acquire_with_cx() and acquire_owned_with_cx() methods that accept
  an explicit Cx, complementing the existing acquire()/acquire_owned()
  that use cx::for_request()

mux_pool.rs:
- Add MuxPoolError coverage for Pool(Cancelled): verify it is not
  classified as timeout and not classified as disconnected
- Add 2 Cx cancellation tests: pre-cancelled list_panes_with_cx
  (verifies no connections created/failed), and pre-cancelled
  health_check_with_cx (verifies failure tracking without connection)

wezterm.rs:
- Exclude PoolError::Cancelled from circuit breaker triggers — a
  cancelled request is a caller decision, not a connection health signal
- Add test verifying Cancelled does not trigger circuit breaker

proptest_pool.rs:
- Add Cancelled to arb_pool_error() generator
- Add prop_error_variants_distinct assertion for Cancelled
- Add prop_cancelled_display_mentions_cancelled property test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
